ogTitle: Swansea Heads vs Sydenham: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Swansea Heads and Sydenham.
heading: Swansea Heads vs Sydenham — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Swansea Heads, New South Wales and Sydenham, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Sydenham has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Swansea Heads (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Swansea Heads or Sydenham safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Swansea Heads has a flood risk score of 29/100, while Sydenham scores 21/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Swansea Heads scores 51/100 for bushfire risk. Sydenham scores 28/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Swansea Heads or Sydenham?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Sydenham has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.58% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
